titleStmt.title: SII 25.114: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 114. (A.R. No. 114 of 1908.) TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: North wall of the kitchen in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.
This inscription may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 13th Century.

It records the gift of 2 nāḻi of rice daily to the deity Aḻaguḍaipiḷḷaiyār in the temple of Kayilāsmuḍaiya-ṉāyaṉār of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu by a certain Veṇṇivēlāṇ of Mutikaṭṭinallūr alias Prathimādēvinallūr in Madurōdaya-vaḷanāḍu. The donor is stated to have deposited 30 śōḻiya-naṟ-paḻaṅ-kāśu in the treasury of the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍa-nāyaṉār to meet the above endowment and the expenses connected with the repairs of the prākāra (tirunaḍai-māḷigai) in the temple.

The king has been identified with his namesake who ascended the throne in 1401 A.D. But the palaeography will miltilate against this identification. Of the three Vikrama Pāṇḍyas who had the accession dates 1249, 1269 and 1283, the first date on which Vikrama Pāṇḍya I with the praśasti Tirumalarmādu, etc., may be the king to whose 13th regnal year our record can be assigned.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrī vikramapāṇṭiya devaṟku yāṇṭu 10 2vatu keraḷa
ciṅkavaḷanāṭṭu brahmadeyan tirupputtūr śrī kayilāsamuṭaiya ṉāyaṉārkku
maturai Utayavaḷanāṭṭu kāru mutti kaṭṭi nallūrāna prathimāte
vi nallūr Aṭikaṇ veṇṇi veḷāṉ Eḻuntaruḷuvitta Aḻa
kuṭaiya piḷḷaiyāṟku nittam Irunāḻi Arici candrāditya vaṟ celvatāka Ivveṇ
ṇiveḷāṉ tiruttaḷiyāṇṭaṉāyanā toṭikkiṉa coḻiya naṟpaḻaṅkācu 3 10
Ikkācu muppatum tirunaṭaimāḷikai tiruppaṇikku I śrī paṇṭāratte celvatāka
Digital edition of SII 25.114 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
